name: core.openwop.triggers
description: Spec-canonical workflow-trigger pack. Four universal entry-point shapes — webhook, schedule, event, envelope — that hosts can use to register workflows for external-event-driven dispatch. Trigger nodes are pass-through executors: they forward `ctx.triggerData` (set by the engine when the trigger fires) to outputs. The pack contributes the typed config + payload schemas + the runtime pass-through. Domain-specific triggers (canvas-aware, chat-aware) live in vendor packs.
author: OpenWOP Working Group

nodes.label: Webhook Trigger
nodes.description: HTTP webhook entry-point. Host's webhook subsystem routes incoming requests at the configured path/secret to this workflow and exposes the body, headers, query, and method via ctx.triggerData. Replay-safe: triggerData is captured at run start and never re-fetched.

nodes.label: Schedule Trigger
nodes.description: Cron-style scheduled entry-point. Host's scheduler fires the workflow at config.cron expressions; ctx.triggerData carries the fire timestamp, the matched cron expression, and timezone metadata. Drift / missed-fire handling is host policy.

nodes.label: Event Trigger
nodes.description: Generic event-subscription entry-point. Host's event bus dispatches to the workflow when a matching event name fires; ctx.triggerData carries the event name + payload + source. For envelope-typed events with schema validation, use core.openwop.triggers.envelope.

nodes.label: Envelope Trigger
nodes.description: Typed-envelope entry-point. Host dispatches when a matching envelope (e.g., from a parent workflow's emit or a typed event publisher) fires. ctx.triggerData carries the envelope's `envelopeType` discriminator + the payload. Workflow authors declare the expected envelope type in config; the host's envelope-routing layer enforces.
